label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the 1974 World Bank study and what it concluded regarding economic growth in some backward countries. The options are A to D. First, I need to go back to the passage and find the relevant part. The passage mentions that a major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that "it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, wait, that wording is a bit tricky. Let me read it again. The exact sentence is: "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So, the study says that decades of growth have been of little benefit to a third of the population. That means two thirds of the population may have benefited? Or is it that only a third benefited? Hmm. Wait, the phrasing is "of little benefit to a third of their population." So, if the growth was of little benefit to a third, that implies that a third didn't get much benefit. But what about the rest? The passage also says later that "increase in GNP per capita did not promise important improvements in such development indicators... its benefit came down to only a small part of the population." So the World Bank study says that even though there was growth, a third of the population didn't benefit much. Therefore, the answer would be that the benefit was limited to a third, meaning the rest (two thirds) did get some benefit, but the question is about what the study found regarding the growth's effect. Looking at the options: (A) benefit to a third of their population (B) benefit to two thirds of their population (C) little benefit to their people (D) no benefit at all to their people The study says that the growth was of little benefit to a third of their population. That doesn't mean that the other two thirds didn't benefit at all. It just says that a third didn't benefit much. So the correct answer is (A)? Wait, but the question is phrased as "brought _".
